entities.name: WATER HYACINTH
entities.description: An invasive weed species (Pontederia crassipes) that causes significant adverse economic and ecological impacts in tropical and sub-tropical regions.

entities.name: TROPICAL AND SUB-TROPICAL REGIONS
entities.description: Geographic areas where water hyacinth infestation is particularly prevalent and impactful.

entities.name: REAL-TIME MONITORING
entities.description: The large-scale tracking of chronic infestation areas to facilitate the formulation of effective control strategies.

entities.name: CONTROL STRATEGIES
entities.description: Management plans designed to mitigate the spread and impact of invasive weed species.

entities.name: REVENUE GENERATION POTENTIAL
entities.description: The economic possibility of deriving value from the harvested biomass of water hyacinth.

entities.name: BIOMASS YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity of water hyacinth to produce biomass within a specific water body.

entities.name: REMOTE SENSING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies used to understand, monitor, and estimate the extent of water hyacinth infestation in inland and coastal freshwater bodies.

entities.name: SATELLITE IMAGERY
entities.description: High spectral, temporal, and spatial resolution data used for the automated analysis of vegetation.

entities.name: MACHINE LEARNING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Computational methods used for the automated image analysis to discriminate water hyacinth from other vegetation.

entities.name: AUTOMATED IMAGE ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of using satellite imagery and machine learning to identify and differentiate water hyacinth infestation.

entities.name: AERIAL SURVEYS
entities.description: Technology-based monitoring methods that complement remote sensing for comprehensive data collection.

entities.name: GROUND-LEVEL SENSORS
entities.description: Technology-based monitoring tools used to complement remote sensing for accurate monitoring.

entities.name: CITIZEN SCIENCE
entities.description: A collaborative monitoring approach involving public participation to complement remote sensing.

entities.name: MULTI-MODAL APPROACH
entities.description: A novel framework that combines remote sensing with aerial surveys, ground-level sensors, and citizen science.
